title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Runtime Contract: Upload Intent State Machine
summary: A database row says ready before S3 accepts bytes, an object exists without an owner row after rollback, and events race ahead of scan and authorization state. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the upload intent state machine increment in file metadata and object reconciliation state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Upload Intent State Machine · Theory Upload Intent State Machine
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8066 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
